name: Okrokana Residence
desc: A hillside family residence above Tbilisi where raw concrete ceilings meet warm oak and gallery walls. Generous glazing pulls the Okrokana landscape into every room, and a roof terrace with an outdoor lounge extends summer living. The children's rooms and master suite each carry a palette of their own.

name: Park Home Apartment
desc: A city apartment built around a green velvet living landscape and a web of black steel lighting. Behind calm oak joinery hides a full family program — a work niche, open shelving displays and stone bathrooms with green ceramic accents.

name: Dadiani Apartment
desc: A characterful apartment where deep green cabinetry, burgundy accents and brass details play against softly washed walls. The dining table doubles as a library desk, and the bathroom turns the palette up with a wine-red ceiling over checkered tile.

name: Jikia Apartment
desc: A light-filled family apartment in pastel tones — an olive sofa, a pale timber kitchen and art-splashed walls. The compact bathroom makes the statement: oxblood red over patterned cement tiles.

name: Private Apartment
desc: A serene apartment in layered creams and greys: curved plaster details, a bedroom wrapped in upholstered panels and a corridor display wall that works like a private gallery. Soft green textiles keep it warm and lived-in.

name: Apartment Design
desc: A complete apartment design in warm minimalism — glass partitions zone the living space, sculptural pendants float above linen seating, and the bedrooms pair textured feature walls with quiet, indirect light.

name: Retail Designs
desc: Retail environments with a theatrical streak: oversized botanical installations over raw concrete, black-and-gold display counters and jewel-box lighting composed for beauty and fashion brands.

name: Café Praline
desc: A patisserie in cream and pistachio: glass-block partitions, globe pendants and botanical murals. The long pastry counter anchors the room, while an amber velvet corner glows after dark.

name: Tourist Agency Office
desc: A travel agency designed in two voices — one soft and residential with blush seating beneath departure boards, one crisp and corporate with ring chandeliers and acoustic clouds. Both are built around an open consulting floor.

name: Bakery Tortiko
desc: A neighbourhood bakery in honey-toned wood with a hand-lettered logo wall — and a display counter designed to put the morning pastries on stage.

name: Office Design
desc: A compact office with a hospitality heart — terracotta pendants, oak-lined meeting niches and travertine washrooms that feel anything but corporate.

name: Dental Clinic
desc: A clinic that calms rather than intimidates: soft grey volumes, a sculptural reception desk, indigo accents and daylight reaching every treatment room.

name: Living Room × Kitchen
desc: A living room and kitchen concept created in partnership with Comforter — a graphite island with brass wire chairs, set against travertine surfaces and warm, focused light.

name: Private Apartment
desc: A generous apartment for a private client — champagne tones, patterned oak floors and couture detailing. Sliding glass partitions offer two ways to live with the kitchen: open, or hidden away.

name: Private House
desc: A pitched-roof family house in Saguramo: a double-height lounge with a marble fireplace wall, mirrored display niches and bedrooms tucked under the timber roofline.

name: Café at Ski Resort
desc: A concept for a ski-resort café — rough stone walls, glossy black tables and a neon line dancing over the bar. Warm timber ceilings set the scene for the après-ski hour.
